summary: Crisp soundboard mastering captures a peak‑era 2/14/70 show with a smoking Dark Star, The Eleven and St. Stephen/Lovelight sandwich.
review: The Miller‑remastered soundboard tape is clear and “extra crispy,” though the early set loses the opening of The Eleven. Musically the performance is hailed as one of the band’s finest February ’70 nights – explosive post‑drums, fluid Jerry solos and standout jams on Dark Star, St. Stephen/Lovelight, Hard to Handle and Not Fade Away.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive fan commentary (most 5‑star) praises both the show’s historic performance and the high‑quality soundboard mastering; only minor recording gaps keep it just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Charlie Miller – crisp, well‑balanced mix. Early set is missing the very start of The Eleven and a William Tell bridge, but overall clarity is excellent.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑part concert: an early, concise set with the classic Dawk Staw > Stephen > Eleven/Lovelight sequence, followed by a late‑night blowout where the band rides high energy through Hard to Handle, Dark Hollow, acoustic gems and an electric marathon culminating in a superb Dark Star and Not Fade Away.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fluid, soaring solos (especially on Dark Star) and “orbiting” melodic ideas; described as the most on‑point guitarist of the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned trying out ideas that dovetailed with Jerry’s playing; solid but not highlighted as a standout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for picking up themes, contributing strong rhythm work and vocal harmonies throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers described as “on point” with an explosive post‑drums feel that drove the jams.
